export_result.sh line 26 and setup_task.sh line 18 both reference this exact path; directory created and managed by task scripts
export_result.sh line 53 checks for 'truck|lorry|road'; USLCI contains 324 on-road trucking datasets per GitHub FLCAC documentation
export_result.sh line 56 checks for 'rail|train|locomotive'; USLCI contains 11 class 1 U.S. rail transport processes per GitHub FLCAC documentation
export_result.sh line 56 checks for 'rail|train|locomotive' as variants of rail keyword

export_result.sh line 59 checks for 'global warming|gwp|climate|co2'; Global Warming is a verified TRACI 2.1 impact category (Global warming - kg CO2 eq) per Earthster Knowledge Base docs.earthster.org/en/articles/8177457-traci-2-1-impact-categories

Metadata claims 'acidification' as required keyword, but export_result.sh (lines 50-64) does NOT check for acidification. Only checks truck, rail, and global warming. However, Acidification is a real TRACI 2.1 impact category (Acidification - kg SO2 eq) per Earthster Knowledge Base. The verifier.py FINAL_STATE_PROMPT (line 62) mentions checking for 'Acidification' in VLM verification, but the export script does not include it in KEYWORDS_FOUND string.
verifier.py line 110: 'if ps_count >= 2:' grants 20 points; this directly matches metadata value
verifier.py line 121: 'if project_count >= 1:' grants 25 points; pass condition on line 172 requires 'project_count >= 1'

install_openlca.sh line 167-198 documents USLCI as real publicly available U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database from NREL; downloaded from GitHub FLCAC repository; contains 324+ on-road truck processes and 11 rail transport processes

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available life cycle inventory and impact assessment data. USLCI is the U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL/FLCAC, containing 324+ trucking datasets and 11 rail transport processes. TRACI v2.1 is the EPA's Tool for Reduction and Assessment of Chemicals and Other Environmental Impacts, with 10 impact categories including Global warming and Acidification. Both datasets are downloaded from authoritative public sources during environment setup and copied to the specified import directories. The task requires creating 2 product systems (truck and rail) and 1 OpenLCA Project, with results exported as CSV containing keywords verified against the export script checks. However, there is a discrepancy: 'acidification' is listed as a required keyword in metadata but is not verified by the export script (only truck, rail, and global warming are checked).
